summary:
Seals prevent recurring cavities around previous fillings but require ongoing checks; they act like a barrier stopping bacteria near these areas which can lead to more problems if not monitored regularly. The main focus is keeping those spots protected so any issues get caught early before becoming bigger ones needing extra work later down the line. To summarize:

Sealants primarily reduce recurrent caries by protecting vulnerable sites post-treatment, requiring periodic assessments to maintain efficacy and catch potential failures promptly. This approach ensures continuous prevention rather than addressing larger future complications arising due to unchecked recurrences.
